title: Proclamation No. 202, s. 1937
text:
MALACAÑAN PALACE MANILA

PROCLAMATION NO. 202

ESTABLISHING AS CENTRAL CEBU NATIONAL PARK FOR THE BENEFIT AND ENJOYMENT OF THE PEOPLE OF THE PHILIPPINES THE PARCEL OF LAND SITUATED IN THE MUNICIPALITIES OF BALAMBAN, TOLEDO, AND THE CITY OF CEBU, PROVINCE OF CEBU, ISLAND OF CEBU.

Upon the recommendation of the Secretary of Agriculture and Commerce, and pursuant to the provisions of section one of Act Numbered Thirty-nine hundred and fifteen, entitled “An Act providing for the establishment of national parks, declaring such parks as game refuges, and for other purposes,” I, Manuel L. Quezon, President of the Philippines, hereby reserve for park purposes for the benefit and enjoyment of the people of the Philippines and withdraw from sale, settlement, or other disposition, subject to private rights, if any there be, the following parcel of the public domain situated in the municipality of Balamban, Toledo, and the City of Cebu, Province of Cebu, Island of Cebu, and described in the Bureau of Forestry map No. N. P. 17, to wit:
